channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Overflowing bins - repeated problem, 22nd March
item.description:
These bins have been reported as overflowing on several different occasions. Could the Education and Enforcement Team or the Estates Recycling Team look at this issue please.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Wilton Way, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Glass shards on pavement & road, 22nd March
item.description:
Still lotsnof broken glass on pavement and road. Dangerous for pedestrians and animals. This area and a little bit up the road too. Would uou please clean up asap? Thank you very much!

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Holmdale Terrace, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Paving stone sunk and lose, just before zebra crossing on old pub side, 22nd March
item.description:
Paving stone sunk and lose, trip hazard at night

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kilburn Lane, Kilburn, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Albany Street mostly & Redhill Street, 22nd March
item.description:
There are no road signs to state “NO Horns/ Honking” There is a significant increase of cars honking throughout the day and night even when there are no cars of pedestrians - it has become the norm like India and a habit. If fines are introduced Camden council can make a lot of money and use it towards improving roads and pavements and on other much needed purpose. Also the speed at which the cars are driven are still very At dual carriage and motorway speeds.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Albany Street, Fitzrovia, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Clean up in Alley ASAP, 22nd March
item.description:
Lots of litter and rubbish around the alley way

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Edgebury Walk, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Panel on the pavement outside 102 Briset Rd, 22nd March
item.description:
Panel on the pavement outside 102 Briset Rd

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Briset Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Parked on payment, 22nd March
item.description:
No room for pedestrians had to walk on the road . Had a pram and it was dangerous

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1308 - 1310, High Road, Whetstone, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken fence on the pavement outside 541 Westhorne Ave, 22nd March
item.description:
Broken fence on the pavement outside 541 Westhorne Ave.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Westhorne Avenue, Eltham

item.title: Litter bins full to overflowing, 22nd March
item.description:
See above

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Thicket Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: More builders rubble and scaffolding dumped infront of Hardcastle House on 22 March, 22nd March
item.description:
Thank you for removing the builders rubbish and rubble for job number 9156075. I am saddened to update that as soon as this was removed the same culprits bought more builders rubble and scaffolding and dumped it infront of Hardcastle House (Oliver Goldsmith Estate, SE15 5TA) again. Please see image attached. After speaking with neighbours it was identified that this rubbish is being bought by vans and cars and looks to be someone doing home renovations and their builders and workers (identified as European men) are dumping here instead of hiring a skip. Furthermore, glass has been dumped on the walkway and poses a risk to residents and children. Debris from the initial dump I contacted you about has also damaged my neighbours car (see silver car in first set of images). Please kindly deal with this imminently, removing the fly tipping and please can a site visit be conducted, speak to residents, install necessary cctv, signage and patrol wardens etc to catch the persons responsible. This is ongoing and our home has been identified by builders as a dump site which it absolutely is not. We have a right to live with dignity and safety. We pay our service charge, rent and council tax and would like to be able to access our homes and building and cars safely and live in a clean, safe, risk free environment. Some neighbours have been fearful to confront the men as they are a big intimidating group. Kind regards Khadijah

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Marmont Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Shopping trolley full of rubbish, 22nd March
item.description:
Shopping trolley abandoned who has then been filled up with rubbish. In the grassy area between Farwig Lane and Weston Grove.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Farwig Lane, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Log posts, 22nd March
item.description:
The log posts holding the bank secure has broken away causing cars and lorries to hit them. As it’s just off of Poverest junction and quite narrow it’s extremely dangerous.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 57, Sutherland Avenue, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Near number 1 Kelly Street, 22nd March
item.description:
Street light not lit at night

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kelly Street, Kentish Town, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Human faeces, 22nd March
item.description:
Been reported many many times. I just keep being told they’ll clean it on their next weekly visit and send me a link to volunteer to clean it, but it’s been there for over 6 weeks! I’ll clean it when I’m not paying council tax for clean streets. It needs to be cleaned asap. I’m reporting it to local councillor
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Rippolson Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping sofas, 22nd March
item.description:
Sofas and palettes flytipped.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lorrimore Square, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
There’s a dead fox lying under my car

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Salusbury Road, London

item.category: Street cleaning - Dead animal on street
